15? Reported by, among others, al-Bukhari (420), Muslim (1794) (and the wording is his), from Ibn Mas'‘td.

58 Authentic, narrated by al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i and al-Hakim, from Anas, and authenticated in Sahih al-Jami # 6275.

The Prophet (#%) used to avoid prolonged, useless speech, for ‘A’ishah narrates that the Prophet (3) “...used to love concise du’ as, and he would leave all (du' as) besides those”.!”

Al-Khattabi states: “So let him (i.e. the worshipper) choose for his du‘ a the appropriate words, and let him praise his Lord using the best praises, and the noblest of them, and the most comprehensive in meaning. For (du'@) is a secret conversation between the slave and the Master of all masters, whom no one resembles, nor does He have an equal!"

Many such da’ ds have been preserved for us in the books of hadith. For example, Farwah ibn Nawfal reported that he asked ‘A’ishah for a du‘a that the Prophet (#%) used to pray with. She answered: “He (38) used to say:

This du‘a is a general, comprehensive one which seeks refuge in Allah from all evil, whether a person has done that evil or not.

Another comprehensive du‘ a that the Prophet (#8) used is:

“O Allah! Forgive my sins and my ignorance, and my transgressions in my affairs, and all that You know of me. O Allah! Forgive me my sins done seriously or jokingly, purposely or unintentionally, and all else that is with me! O Allah, forgive me what I have done, and what I have yet to do, and what I have done in secret, and what I have done openely, and all that you know of me. You are the First, and you are the Last, and You are capable of all things!"

159 Authentic, narrated by Abu Dawid, Ahmad, Ibn Hibban and others, from ‘A’ishah, and authenticated in Sahih al-Jami' # 4949

161 Narrated by Muslim from Abu Dharr, and Abt Dawid and al-Nasa’i from ‘A’ishah, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jami’ # 1293.

to Reported by al-Bukhari and Muslim, from Aba Masa al-Ash‘ari’, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jami’ # 1264.

And from such concise prayers is the du’ a of the Prophet (3%):

“O Allah, Our Lord! Grant us the good in this world, and the good in the Hereafter, and save us from the punishment of Hell!"

Therefore, remember the value of your time, and do not cease to make du'@ to Allah on all occasions and as often as you can. And how many are the words that spring forth from our mouths, that will not benefit us at all, or even be used against us on the Day of Judgement (we seek refuge in Allah from that!). Yet, how easy these simple du’ as are, and how concise and brief, and how full of blessings and mercy! For is it not possible that a person says one of these concise du‘ ds, thinking it a small and trivial request, and yet the angels of Mercy take it, and the doors of Heaven are opened for it, and the Lord of Mercy responds to it, so that on the Day of Judgement, the Scales weigh heavy because of it?

And how true the Arabic proverb: the best speech is that which is concise yet clear in meaning.

16. To Start dua with One’s Self

It is desirable that the person making the dua should first ask of it for himself. This is so for a number of reasons. Firstly, a person should desire all good for himself, so it does not make sense to pray for others and forget one’s self. Secondly, the fact that a person prays for himself shows that what he is praying for is an important goal . and objective. In other words, this ensures that he desires for his brothers what he desires for himself. Thirdly, it increases him in his sincerity in this du‘a, for verily mankind is weak, so when he makes du'‘a only for his brother, it is possible that it will not be as sincere as when he makes a du‘ a for himself and his brother.

This principle is demonstrated in a number of Qur’anic verses, such as:

‘63 Reported by al-Bukhari and Muslim, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jami' # 1306.

oe hee e.cr rtyse SAAC MOPS CI aH “O Allah! Forgive us, and our brothers (in faith) who have

5 A ables dE

“And he (Musa) prayed: O my Lord! Forgive me and my

brother, and enter us into your Mercy!”

CY Sl oodl peo ie HU Gays LeIS

“O Our Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and all of the be-

“And Seek forgiveness (O Muhammad) for your sins, and for

the believing men and women.

However, this was not the constant habit of the Prophet (#8), for there are other narrations to show that sometimes he (3%) would pray for another person without praying for himself. For example, when he was speaking about Hajar, Isma‘il’s mother , he (38) said:

“May Allah have mercy on the mother of Isma‘il. Had she left

have been a clear lake”. 164 Surah al-Hashr, 10. 165 Sarah al-A’ raf, 151. 166 Sarah Ibrahim, 41. 187 Sarah Muhammad, 19.

168 Authentic, narrated by Abu Dawid, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i and others,

from Ubayy ibn Ka‘b, and authenticated in Sahih al-Jami # 4723.

19 Reported by al-Bukhari, from Anas ibn Malik, as reported in Sahih al-

He also said regarding Hassan ibn Thabit, “O Allah! Help him with the Holy Spirit (the Angel Jibril)!”,'” and for Ibn ‘Abbas,

“O Allah! Grant him an understanding of the religion!”"”!

These, and other examples besides these, show the permissibility of making a du‘ a without mentioning one’s self, but in general it is recommended to do so.

17. To Pray for AJl Muslims

Part of the completeness of one’s iman is that a person loves for his brother what he loves for himself. Therefore, just as he desires that he be guided to the truth, and be forgiven for his sins, so too should he desire the same for his fellow Muslims.

The Prophet (3%) is commanded in the Qur’an to:

Ae ssttee Th fae, 70 oe

...seek forgiveness (from Allah) for your sins, and the sins of the believing men and women.

Likewise, the Prophet Nuh prayed to Allah:

“O My Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and whoever entered my house as a believer, and all the believing men and

Reported by al-Bukhari (453), Muslim (2485) and others, from Abu Hurayrah.

17 Reported by al-Bukhari (143), Muslim (2477) and others, from Ibn ‘Abbas. 122 Sarah Muhammad, 19. '3 sarah Nah, 28.

And the Prophet (3%) said:

“Whoever seeks forgiveness for the believing men and women, then a good deed will be written for him for every single be-

lieving man and women (that he prayed for)”. "4

So it is encouraged for the Muslim to remember all of his brothers and sisters when he is making a du'@, and to pray for the entire Muslim ummah. He should also pray against the enemies of Islam, against tyrants and non-Muslim rulers that fight and torture the Muslims. All of this is a sign of the person’s own iman, and is contained within the etiquette of dua.

Of particular importance is that a person prays for his own parents (if they are alive, or if they died as Muslims). Allah specifically instructs the believers in this respect,

155.85 peso See ash, Gao nS

And lower unto them the wings of humility and mercy, and say, ‘O My Lord! Have mercy on them, even as they took care

of me while I was young.”

The dua of Nuh has already been given above, in which he prayed for his parents, as did Ibrahim:

OY Sl Coll Aso Cas Weel

4 Reported by al-Tabarani in his a/-Kadir, and al-Haythami said in Maja’ al-Zawa'id: “Its isnad (chain of transmission) is good.” Al-Albani reported this statement, but did not pronounce a verdict on the hadith himself. See Sahih al-Jami', # 6026.

1% Sarah al-Isra, 24. 1% seerah Ibrahim, 41.

18. To Say ‘Amin’

When a person is listening to another person make du'4, it is recommended that he say, ‘Amin’, which means ‘O Allah! Accept (or: Respond to) this du’ a.’

It is stated that the phrase ‘Amin’ is one of the specialities of this nation, and that no nation before Islam had been given it.” This is inferred from the hadith in which the Prophet (3) said:

The Jews do not envy you for any matter as much as they envy you for Amin, and for giving salams to one another.

The purpose of saying ‘Amin’ is so that it might increase the chances that a person’s dua be answered. It is like repeating the dua over again, except that instead of going into all the details of one’s du’, it is all summarised in the one phrase, ‘Amin,’ which means, as we mentioned, ‘O Allah! Respond (to the du‘ a)!’ So it is like another du'@ after the first one.

Of course, there are certain du‘ as that should be done privately, not in a group, and there are others that are done in a group. Examples of the first type are most of the du’ as that a person does during the day and night. It is an innovation to have a group du ‘@ for, say, entering the house, or after finishing the salah. An example of the second type is the dz‘ @ that is done in gunat, where the imam makes a du a, and the congregation behind him says, ‘Amin.’ Likewise, if the person giving the Friday sermon makes a du‘a, the congregation is encouraged to say ‘Amin’ after his du'@. Furthermore to say - ‘Amin’ after reciting Surah al-Fatihah is encouraged, regardless of

177 Another opinion is that Misa and Harun were taught this phrase, but the

Jews were not informed of this; this opinion is due to the story that follows shortly. (See Aba Zayd, pps. 203-209, for a complete discussion of ‘Amin’.) However, this opinion clashes with the well-known fact that the Christians to this day use this phrase (as Amen) after their prayers, so further research needs to be done on this issue: did the Christians take it from the Muslims, or was this phrase given to nations before us as well?
content_id:
15? Dilaporkan oleh, antara lain, al-Bukhari (420), Muslim (1794) (dan lafaznya adalah miliknya), dari Ibn Mas'ud.

58 Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i dan al-Hakim, dari Anas, dan disahihkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 6275.

Nabi (ﷺ) terbiasa menghindari ucapan panjang yang tidak berguna, karena 'A'ishah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) "...menyukai du'a yang ringkas, dan beliau meninggalkan semua (du'a) selain dari itu".!"

Al-Khattabi menyatakan: "Maka hendaklah ia (yakni, orang yang beribadah) memilih bagi du'anya kata-kata yang tepat, dan hendaklah ia memuji Tuhannya dengan pujian terbaik, dan yang paling mulia di antaranya, dan yang paling komprehensif maknanya. Karena (du'a) adalah percakapan rahasia antara hamba dan Tuan dari segala tuan, yang tidak ada yang menyerupai-Nya, dan Dia tidak memiliki tandingan!"

Banyak du'a seperti itu telah terpelihara bagi kita dalam kitab-kitab hadith. Misalnya, Farwah ibn Nawfal melaporkan bahwa ia bertanya kepada 'A'ishah tentang du'a yang biasa dipanjatkan Nabi (ﷺ). Ia menjawab: "Beliau (ﷺ) biasa berkata:

Du'a ini adalah du'a yang umum dan menyeluruh yang memohon perlindungan kepada Allah dari segala kejahatan, baik seseorang telah melakukan kejahatan itu maupun tidak.

Du'a komprehensif lainnya yang biasa digunakan Nabi (ﷺ) adalah:

"Ya Allah! Ampunilah dosa-dosaku dan kejahilanku, dan pelanggaranku dalam urusan-urusanku, dan semua yang Engkau ketahui tentang diriku. Ya Allah! Ampunilah aku atas dosa-dosa yang kulakukan secara serius maupun bercanda, secara sengaja maupun tidak sengaja, dan segala yang ada padaku! Ya Allah, ampunilah aku atas apa yang telah kulakukan, dan apa yang belum kulakukan, dan apa yang kulakukan secara tersembunyi, dan apa yang kulakukan secara terbuka, dan semua yang Engkau ketahui tentang diriku. Engkaulah Yang Pertama, dan Engkaulah Yang Terakhir, dan Engkau Mahakuasa atas segala sesuatu!"

159 Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud, Ahmad, Ibn Hibban dan lainnya, dari 'A'ishah, dan disahihkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 4949

161 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dari Abu Dharr, dan Abu Dawud dan al-Nasa'i dari 'A'ishah, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 1293.

162 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim, dari Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 1264.

Dan termasuk du'a yang ringkas adalah du'a Nabi (ﷺ):

"Ya Allah, Tuhan kami! Berikanlah kepada kami kebaikan di dunia ini, dan kebaikan di Akhirat, dan selamatkanlah kami dari siksa neraka!"

Oleh karena itu, ingatlah nilai waktumu, dan janganlah berhenti memanjatkan du'a kepada Allah dalam setiap kesempatan dan sesering mungkin. Dan betapa banyak kata-kata yang keluar dari mulut kita, yang tidak akan memberi manfaat apa pun bagi kita, atau bahkan akan digunakan melawan kita pada Hari Kiamat (kita berlindung kepada Allah dari hal itu!). Namun, betapa mudahnya du'a-du'a sederhana ini, dan betapa ringkas dan singkatnya, serta betapa penuh berkah dan rahmat! Bukankah mungkin seseorang mengucapkan salah satu du'a ringkas ini, menganggapnya permintaan kecil dan sepele, namun para malaikat Rahmat mengambilnya, dan pintu-pintu Surga dibuka untuknya, dan Tuhan Yang Maha Penyayang meresponsnya, sehingga pada Hari Kiamat, timbangan menjadi berat karenanya?

Dan betapa benarnya pepatah Arab: sebaik-baik perkataan adalah yang ringkas namun jelas maknanya.

16. Memulai Du'a dengan Diri Sendiri

Dianjurkan bagi orang yang berdoa untuk terlebih dahulu memintanya bagi dirinya sendiri. Hal ini demikian karena beberapa alasan. Pertama, seseorang hendaknya menginginkan segala kebaikan bagi dirinya sendiri, sehingga tidak masuk akal untuk berdoa bagi orang lain dan melupakan diri sendiri. Kedua, fakta bahwa seseorang berdoa untuk dirinya sendiri menunjukkan bahwa apa yang ia doakan adalah tujuan dan sasaran yang penting. Dengan kata lain, hal ini memastikan bahwa ia menginginkan bagi saudara-saudaranya apa yang ia inginkan bagi dirinya sendiri. Ketiga, hal itu meningkatkan keikhlasannya dalam du'a ini, karena sesungguhnya manusia itu lemah, sehingga ketika ia berdoa hanya untuk saudaranya, ada kemungkinan du'anya tidak akan seikhlasnya seperti ketika ia berdoa untuk dirinya sendiri dan saudaranya.

Prinsip ini didemonstrasikan dalam sejumlah ayat al-Qur'an, seperti:

'63 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 1306.

"Ya Allah! Ampunilah kami, dan saudara-saudara kami (seiman) yang telah

"Dan ia (Musa) berdoa: Ya Tuhanku! Ampunilah aku dan saudaraku, dan masukkanlah kami ke dalam rahmat-Mu!"

"Ya Tuhan kami! Ampunilah aku, dan kedua orang tuaku, dan seluruh

"Dan mohonlah ampunan (wahai Muhammad) atas dosa-dosamu, dan bagi laki-laki dan perempuan yang beriman."

Namun demikian, ini bukanlah kebiasaan tetap Nabi (ﷺ), karena ada riwayat-riwayat lain yang menunjukkan bahwa terkadang beliau (ﷺ) berdoa untuk orang lain tanpa berdoa untuk dirinya sendiri. Misalnya, ketika beliau berbicara tentang Hajar, ibu Isma'il, beliau (ﷺ) bersabda:

"Semoga Allah merahmati ibu Isma'il. Andai ia membiarkan

akan menjadi sebuah danau yang jernih". 164 Surah al-Hashr, 10. 165 Surah al-A'raf, 151. 166 Surah Ibrahim, 41. 167 Surah Muhammad, 19.

168 Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i dan lainnya, dari Ubayy ibn Ka'b, dan disahihkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 4723.

169 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari, dari Anas ibn Malik, sebagaimana dilaporkan dalam Sahih al-

Beliau juga berkata mengenai Hassan ibn Thabit, "Ya Allah! Bantulah dia dengan Ruh Kudus (Malaikat Jibril)!",'" dan untuk Ibn 'Abbas,

"Ya Allah! Berikanlah kepadanya pemahaman tentang agama!"""!

Contoh-contoh ini, dan contoh-contoh lainnya selain dari ini, menunjukkan kebolehan memanjatkan du'a tanpa menyebut diri sendiri, namun secara umum dianjurkan untuk melakukannya.

17. Berdoa untuk Seluruh Kaum Muslimin

Bagian dari kesempurnaan iman seseorang adalah bahwa ia mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang ia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri. Oleh karena itu, sebagaimana ia menginginkan dirinya dibimbing kepada kebenaran dan diampuni dosa-dosanya, demikian pula hendaknya ia menginginkan hal yang sama bagi sesama Muslim.

Nabi (ﷺ) diperintahkan dalam al-Qur'an untuk:

...mohonkanlah ampunan (dari Allah) atas dosa-dosamu, dan dosa-dosa laki-laki dan perempuan yang beriman.

Demikian pula, Nabi Nuh berdoa kepada Allah:

"Ya Tuhanku! Ampunilah aku, dan kedua orang tuaku, dan siapa pun yang masuk ke dalam rumahku sebagai orang yang beriman, dan semua laki-laki dan perempuan yang beriman."

Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari (453), Muslim (2485) dan lainnya, dari Abu Hurayrah.

171 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari (143), Muslim (2477) dan lainnya, dari Ibn 'Abbas. 172 Surah Muhammad, 19. 173 Surah Nuh, 28.

Dan Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

"Barangsiapa memohonkan ampunan bagi laki-laki dan perempuan yang beriman, maka akan dituliskan baginya satu kebaikan untuk setiap laki-laki dan perempuan yang beriman (yang ia doakan)". "4

Maka dianjurkan bagi seorang Muslim untuk mengingat semua saudara-saudaranya ketika ia sedang memanjatkan du'a, dan untuk mendoakan seluruh ummah Islam. Ia juga hendaknya berdoa melawan musuh-musuh Islam, melawan para tiran dan penguasa non-Muslim yang memerangi dan menyiksa kaum Muslimin. Semua ini adalah tanda iman seseorang, dan termasuk dalam adab du'a.

Yang sangat penting adalah bahwa seseorang mendoakan kedua orang tuanya sendiri (jika mereka masih hidup, atau jika mereka meninggal sebagai Muslim). Allah secara khusus memerintahkan orang-orang beriman dalam hal ini,

Dan rendahkanlah dirimu terhadap mereka berdua dengan penuh kasih sayang, dan katakanlah, 'Ya Tuhanku! Sayangilah mereka berdua, sebagaimana mereka menyayangiku waktu aku kecil.'

Du'a Nuh telah disebutkan di atas, di mana ia berdoa untuk orang tuanya, sebagaimana Ibrahim juga:

174 Dilaporkan oleh al-Tabarani dalam al-Kabir-nya, dan al-Haythami berkata dalam Majma' al-Zawa'id: "Isnad-nya (rantai periwayatan) adalah baik." Al-Albani melaporkan pernyataan ini, tetapi tidak memberikan keputusan mengenai hadith itu sendiri. Lihat Sahih al-Jami', # 6026.

175 Surah al-Isra', 24. 176 Surah Ibrahim, 41.

18. Mengucapkan 'Amin'

Ketika seseorang mendengarkan orang lain memanjatkan du'a, dianjurkan untuk mengucapkan 'Amin', yang berarti 'Ya Allah! Kabulkanlah (atau: Responlah) du'a ini.'

Disebutkan bahwa lafaz 'Amin' adalah salah satu keistimewaan umat ini, dan bahwa tidak ada umat sebelum Islam yang diberikannya." Hal ini disimpulkan dari hadith di mana Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

Orang-orang Yahudi tidak iri kepada kalian atas suatu perkara pun sebagaimana mereka iri kepada kalian karena Amin, dan karena saling mengucapkan salam satu sama lain.

Tujuan mengucapkan 'Amin' adalah agar hal itu dapat meningkatkan kemungkinan du'a seseorang dikabulkan. Ini seperti mengulang du'a kembali, kecuali bahwa alih-alih masuk ke semua detail du'a-nya, semuanya diringkas dalam satu lafaz, 'Amin,' yang berarti, sebagaimana kami sebutkan, 'Ya Allah! Responlah (du'a tersebut)!' Maka ia bagaikan du'a lain setelah du'a yang pertama.

Tentu saja, ada du'a tertentu yang seharusnya dilakukan secara pribadi, bukan dalam kelompok, dan ada pula yang dilakukan secara berkelompok. Contoh jenis pertama adalah sebagian besar du'a yang dilakukan seseorang sepanjang siang dan malam. Merupakan bid'ah jika ada du'a berjamaah untuk, misalnya, memasuki rumah, atau setelah selesai salah. Contoh jenis kedua adalah du'a yang dilakukan dalam qunut, di mana imam membaca du'a, dan makmum di belakangnya mengucapkan 'Amin.' Demikian pula, jika khatib Jumat membaca du'a, makmum dianjurkan mengucapkan 'Amin' setelah du'a tersebut. Selain itu, mengucapkan 'Amin' setelah membaca Surah al-Fatihah dianjurkan, terlepas dari

177 Pendapat lain menyatakan bahwa Musa dan Harun diajarkan kalimat ini, namun

orang-orang Yahudi tidak diberitahu tentang hal ini; pendapat ini didasarkan pada kisah yang menyusul tak lama kemudian. (Lihat Abu Zayd, hlm. 203-209, untuk pembahasan lengkap tentang 'Amin'.) Namun, pendapat ini bertentangan dengan fakta yang sudah dikenal bahwa umat Kristiani hingga hari ini menggunakan kalimat ini (sebagai Amen) setelah doa-doa mereka, sehingga penelitian lebih lanjut perlu dilakukan mengenai masalah ini: apakah umat Kristiani mengambilnya dari kaum Muslimin, ataukah kalimat ini juga diberikan kepada umat-umat sebelum kita?
content_ar: 15? Reported by, among others, البخاري (420), مسلم (1794) (and the wording is his), from Ibn Mas'‘td. 58 Authentic, narrated by al-الترمذي, al-Nasa’i and al-Hakim, from أنس, and authenticated in صحيح al-Jami # 6275. النبي ﷺ (#%) used to avoid prolonged, useless speech, for ‘A’ishah narrates that النبي ﷺ (3) “...used to المحبة concise du’ as, and he would leave all (du' as) بالإضافة إلى those”.!” Al-Khattabi states: “لذا let him (i.e. the worshipper) choose for his du‘ a the appropriate words, and let him praise his Lord using the best praises, and the noblest of them, and the most comprehensive in meaning. For (du'@) is a secret conversation between the slave and the Master of all masters, whom no one resembles, nor does He have an equal!" Many such da’ ds have been preserved for us in the الكتب of الحديث. على سبيل المثال, Farwah ibn Nawfal reported that he asked ‘A’ishah for a du‘a that النبي ﷺ (#%) used to pray with. She answered: “He (38) used to say: This du‘a is a general, comprehensive one which seeks refuge in الله from all الشر, whether a person has done that الشر or not. Another comprehensive du‘ a that النبي ﷺ (#8) used is: “O الله! Forgive my الذنوب and my ignorance, and my transgressions in my affairs, and all that You know of me. O الله! Forgive me my الذنوب done seriously or jokingly, purposely or unintentionally, and all else that is with me! O الله, forgive me what I have done, and what I have yet to do, and what I have done in secret, and what I have done openely, and all that you know of me. You are the أولاً, and you are the Last, and You are capable of all things!" 159 Authentic, narrated by Abu Dawid, أحمد, Ibn Hibban and others, from ‘A’ishah, and authenticated in صحيح al-Jami' # 4949 161 Narrated by مسلم from Abu Dharr, and Abt Dawid and al-Nasa’i from ‘A’ishah, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jami’ # 1293. to Reported by البخاري and مسلم, from Aba Masa al-Ash‘ari’, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jami’ # 1264. And from such concise الصلوات is the du’ a of النبي ﷺ (3%): “O الله, Our Lord! Grant us the الخير in this الدنيا, and the الخير in الآخرة, and save us from the العقاب of النار!" لذلك, remember the value of your time, and do not cease to make du'@ to الله on all occasions and as often as you can. And how many are the words that spring forth from our mouths, that will not benefit us at all, or even be used against us on the يوم القيامة (we seek refuge in الله from that!). Yet, how easy these simple du’ as are, and how concise and brief, and how full of البركات and الرحمة! For is it not possible that a person says one of these concise du‘ ds, thinking it a small and trivial request, and yet the الملائكة of الرحمة take it, and the doors of Heaven are opened for it, and the Lord of الرحمة responds to it, لذا that on the يوم القيامة, the Scales weigh heavy لأن of it? And how true the Arabic proverb: the best speech is that which is concise yet clear in meaning. 16. To Start الدعاء with One’s Self It is desirable that the person making the الدعاء should أولاً ask of it for himself. This is لذا for a number of reasons. Firstly, a person should desire all الخير for himself, لذا it does not make sense to pray for others and forget one’s self. Secondly, the fact that a person prays for himself shows that what he is praying for is an المهم goal . and objective. بعبارة أخرى, this ensures that he desires for his brothers what he desires for himself. Thirdly, it increases him in his sincerity in this du‘a, for verily mankind is weak, لذا عندما he makes du'‘a only for his الأخ, it is possible that it will not be as sincere as عندما he makes a du‘ a for himself and his الأخ. This principle is demonstrated in a number of Qur’anic الآيات, مثل: ‘63 Reported by البخاري and مسلم, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jami' # 1306. oe hee e.cr rtyse SAAC MOPS CI aH “O الله! Forgive us, and our brothers (in الإيمان) who have 5 A ables dE “And he (موسى ﷺ) prayed: O my Lord! Forgive me and my الأخ, and enter us into your الرحمة!” CY Sl oodl peo ie HU Gays LeIS “O Our Lord! Forgive me, and my الوالدان, and all of the be- “And Seek المغفرة (O محمد ﷺ) for your الذنوب, and for the believing men and women. غير أن, this was not the constant habit of النبي ﷺ (#8), for there are other narrations to show that sometimes he (3%) would pray for another person without praying for himself. على سبيل المثال, عندما he was speaking about Hajar, Isma‘il’s الأم , he (38) said: “May الله have الرحمة on the الأم of Isma‘il. Had she left have been a clear lake”. 164 السورة al-Hashr, 10. 165 Sarah al-A’ raf, 151. 166 Sarah إبراهيم ﷺ, 41. 187 Sarah محمد ﷺ, 19. 168 Authentic, narrated by Abu Dawid, al-الترمذي, al-Nasa’i and others, from Ubayy ibn Ka‘b, and authenticated in صحيح al-Jami # 4723. 19 Reported by البخاري, from أنس ibn مالك, as reported in صحيح al- He أيضاً said فيما يخص Hassan ibn Thabit, “O الله! Help him with the Holy الروح (the Angel جبريل ﷺ)!”,'” and for Ibn ‘Abbas, “O الله! Grant him an الفهم of the religion!”"”! These, and other examples بالإضافة إلى these, show the permissibility of making a du‘ a without mentioning one’s self, but in general يستحب to do لذا. 17. To Pray for AJl المسلمون Part of the completeness of one’s الإيمان is that a person loves for his الأخ what he loves for himself. لذلك, just as he desires that he be guided to the الحق, and be forgiven for his الذنوب, لذا أيضاً should he desire the same for his fellow المسلمون. النبي ﷺ (3%) is commanded in the Qur’an to: Ae ssttee Th fae, 70 oe ...seek المغفرة (from الله) for your الذنوب, and the الذنوب of the believing men and women. Likewise, النبي ﷺ Nuh prayed to الله: “O My Lord! Forgive me, and my الوالدان, and whoever entered my house as a المؤمن, and all the believing men and Reported by البخاري (453), مسلم (2485) and others, from أبو هريرة. 17 Reported by البخاري (143), مسلم (2477) and others, from Ibn ‘Abbas. 122 Sarah محمد ﷺ, 19. '3 sarah Nah, 28. And النبي ﷺ (3%) said: “Whoever seeks المغفرة for the believing men and women, then a الخير deed will be written for him for every single be- lieving man and women (that he prayed for)”. "4 لذا it is encouraged for the مسلم to remember all of his brothers and sisters عندما he is making a du'@, and to pray for the entire مسلم الأمة. He should أيضاً pray against the enemies of الإسلام, against tyrants and non-مسلم rulers that fight and torture the المسلمون. All of this is a sign of the person’s own الإيمان, and is contained within the الآداب of الدعاء. Of particular importance is that a person prays for his own الوالدان (إذا they are alive, or إذا they died as المسلمون). الله specifically instructs the المؤمنون in this الاحترام, 155.85 peso See ash, Gao nS And lower unto them the wings of التواضع and الرحمة, and say, ‘O My Lord! Have الرحمة on them, even as they took care of me بينما I was young.” The الدعاء of Nuh has already been given above, in which he prayed for his الوالدان, as did إبراهيم ﷺ: OY Sl Coll Aso Cas Weel 4 Reported by al-Tabarani in his a/-Kadir, and al-Haythami said in Maja’ al-Zawa'id: “Its الإسناد (chain of transmission) is الخير.” Al-Albani reported this statement, but did not pronounce a verdict on the الحديث himself. See صحيح al-Jami', # 6026. 1% Sarah al-Isra, 24. 1% seerah إبراهيم ﷺ, 41. 18. To Say ‘آمين’ عندما a person is listening to another person make du'4, يستحب that he say, ‘آمين’, أي ‘O الله! Accept (or: Respond to) this du’ a.’ جاء في that the phrase ‘آمين’ is one of the specialities of this nation, and that no nation قبل الإسلام had been given it.” This is inferred from the الحديث in which النبي ﷺ (3) said: The Jews do not الحسد you for any matter as much as they الحسد you for آمين, and for giving salams to بعضهم بعضاً. The purpose of saying ‘آمين’ is لذا that it might increase the chances that a person’s الدعاء be answered. It is like repeating the الدعاء over again, except that instead of going into all the details of one’s du’, it is all summarised in the one phrase, ‘آمين,’ أي, as we mentioned, ‘O الله! Respond (to the du‘ a)!’ لذا it is like another du'@ بعد the أولاً one. Of course, there are certain du‘ as that should be done privately, not in a group, and there are others that are done in a group. Examples of the أولاً type are most of the du’ as that a person does خلال the day and night. It is an البدعة to have a group du ‘@ for, say, entering the house, or بعد finishing the الصلاة. An example of the ثانياً type is the dz‘ @ that is done in gunat, where the الإمام makes a du a, and the congregation behind him says, ‘آمين.’ Likewise, إذا the person giving the Friday sermon makes a du‘a, the congregation is encouraged to say ‘آمين’ بعد his du'@. علاوة على ذلك to say - ‘آمين’ بعد reciting السورة al-Fatihah is encouraged, regardless of 177 Another opinion is that Misa and Harun were taught this phrase, but the Jews were not informed of this; this opinion is بسبب the story that follows shortly. (See Aba Zayd, pps. 203-209, for a complete discussion of ‘آمين’.) غير أن, this opinion clashes with the well-known fact that the Christians to this day use this phrase (as Amen) بعد their الصلوات, لذا further research needs to be done on this issue: did the Christians take it from the المسلمون, or was this phrase given to nations قبل us as well?
